title: cladogynos orientalis
intro: Cladogynos orientalis, also known as the Indian staff vine or wild asparagus, is a perennial climber that belongs to the family Capparaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cladogynos orientalis include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Cladogynos orientalis is often prepared as a herbal tea or powder, and can also be consumed raw or dried to be used in various herbal preparations, such as capsules or tinctures.
